edges.description: Developmental eye defects arise downstream of altered ZNF526 function.
Abnormal brain growth contributes to severe microcephaly.
Cortical developmental abnormality contributes to simplified gyration on neuroimaging.
Brain developmental abnormality contributes to epileptic encephalopathy.
Brain developmental abnormality can contribute to limb hypotonia.
Brain developmental abnormality can contribute to dystonic movements.
Developmental eye abnormalities contribute to bilateral cataracts.

nodes.description: The syndrome prominently affects brain development and produces microcephaly, cortical simplification, and epileptic encephalopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: Our findings support the role of ZNF526 biallelic variants in a complex neurodevelopmental disorder, primarily affecting brain and eyes, resulting in severe microcephaly, simplified gyral pattern, epileptic encephalopathy and bilateral cataracts.

nodes.description: ZNF526 dysfunction perturbs ocular development and is associated with cataract formation.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: Mutant znf526 zebrafish larvae had notable malformations of the eye and central nervous system, resembling findings seen in the human holoprosencephaly spectrum.

nodes.description: Neurology-led antiseizure pharmacotherapy should be individualized to seizure type and severity, recognizing that epileptic encephalopathy can occur.

evidence.reference_title: Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants in ZNF526 Causing Dentici‐Novelli Neurodevelopmental Syndrome: A Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The proband, a 7‐month‐old girl, exhibited developmental delay, microcephaly, limb hypotonia, abnormal brain imaging, and seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: Our findings support the role of ZNF526 biallelic variants in a complex neurodevelopmental disorder, primarily affecting brain and eyes, resulting in severe microcephaly, simplified gyral pattern, epileptic encephalopathy and bilateral cataracts.

nodes.description: Bilateral cataract is a characteristic ocular manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: All of them also displayed bilateral progressive cataracts.

nodes.description: Developmental services and physical therapy should address severe developmental delay, hypotonia, and hypertonic-dystonic movements.

evidence.reference_title: Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants in ZNF526 Causing Dentici‐Novelli Neurodevelopmental Syndrome: A Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The proband, a 7‐month‐old girl, exhibited developmental delay, microcephaly, limb hypotonia, abnormal brain imaging, and seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: We identified five patients from four unrelated families with homozygous ZNF526 variants by whole exome sequencing. Four had variants resulting in truncation of ZNF526; they were affected by severe prenatal and postnatal microcephaly (ranging from -4 SD to -8 SD), profound psychomotor delay, hypertonic-dystonic movements, epilepsy and simplified gyral pattern on MRI.

nodes.description: Hypertonic-dystonic movements are part of the severe movement disorder phenotype in some affected individuals.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: We identified five patients from four unrelated families with homozygous ZNF526 variants by whole exome sequencing. Four had variants resulting in truncation of ZNF526; they were affected by severe prenatal and postnatal microcephaly (ranging from -4 SD to -8 SD), profound psychomotor delay, hypertonic-dystonic movements, epilepsy and simplified gyral pattern on MRI.

nodes.description: Epileptic encephalopathy is part of the severe truncating-variant presentation reported in the syndrome-defining cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: CONCLUSION: Our findings support the role of ZNF526 biallelic variants in a complex neurodevelopmental disorder, primarily affecting brain and eyes, resulting in severe microcephaly, simplified gyral pattern, epileptic encephalopathy and bilateral cataracts.

nodes.description: Early severe developmental delay is a core syndrome feature.

evidence.reference_title: Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants in ZNF526 Causing Dentici‐Novelli Neurodevelopmental Syndrome: A Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Recent studies suggest that biallelic pathogenic variants in ZNF526 may lead to Dentici‐Novelli neurodevelopmental syndrome, characterized by microcephaly, developmental delay, epilepsy, and ocular anomalies.

nodes.description: Limb hypotonia is reported in affected individuals with Dentici-Novelli neurodevelopmental syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants in ZNF526 Causing Dentici‐Novelli Neurodevelopmental Syndrome: A Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The proband, a 7‐month‐old girl, exhibited developmental delay, microcephaly, limb hypotonia, abnormal brain imaging, and seizures.

nodes.description: Severe microcephaly is a major recurring feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: We identified five patients from four unrelated families with homozygous ZNF526 variants by whole exome sequencing. Four had variants resulting in truncation of ZNF526; they were affected by severe prenatal and postnatal microcephaly (ranging from -4 SD to -8 SD), profound psychomotor delay, hypertonic-dystonic movements, epilepsy and simplified gyral pattern on MRI.

nodes.description: Regular ophthalmologic evaluation should monitor progressive bilateral cataracts and guide optical or cataract-directed intervention when clinically indicated.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic variants in ZNF526 cause a severe neurodevelopmental disorder with microcephaly, bilateral cataract, epilepsy and simplified gyration.
evidence.snippet: All of them also displayed bilateral progressive cataracts.

nodes.description: Epilepsy is part of the characteristic neurologic syndrome.

evidence.snippet: The proband, a 7‐month‐old girl, exhibited developmental delay, microcephaly, limb hypotonia, abnormal brain imaging, and seizures.

nodes.description: Simplified gyral pattern on MRI is a core structural brain abnormality in the syndrome.

nodes.description: ZNF526 encodes a Kruppel-type zinc finger protein with transcriptional regulatory function. Biallelic pathogenic variants disrupt this regulatory role and initiate the syndrome.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: Novel Compound Heterozygous Variants in ZNF526 Causing Dentici‐Novelli Neurodevelopmental Syndrome: A Case Report and Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The ZNF526 gene encodes a ubiquitously expressed Kruppel‐type zinc finger protein crucial in transcriptional regulation.
